reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (85 - 47) is simple and I’m certain my answer is correct. In all previous games, everyone’s initial arithmetic answers have been correct and no conflicting capsules appeared. Verifying would cost coins and still risks a random death if nobody volunteers, as happened in Game 1. Volunteering immediately with the single capsule for each question is very likely safe and preserves all 4 of my coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (8 × 9) is trivial, so I’m certain Q2-B is correct. All previous games have been simple arithmetic with zero wrong answers and there is only one capsule per question with no conflicts, so the chance any of the four answers is wrong is extremely small. Verifying would cost a coin and still leave most of the same residual risk, and if everyone hesitates again we risk another no-volunteer round with a 1-in-4 chance of random death, as in Game 1. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my survival chances while keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (56 ÷ 7) is trivial, so I’m certain my own capsule Q3-C is correct. In all prior games, everyone’s arithmetic answers have been correct, and the questions are always very simple, so the chance that any of Q1-A, Q2-B, or Q4-D is wrong is extremely small. Verifying would burn a coin without meaningfully improving safety, while if nobody volunteers we risk another random elimination like in Game 1. Volunteering now with the only available capsules gives an overwhelmingly high chance of immediate escape while keeping all 4 coins.
